root_text.sn47.41:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ;
root_text.sn47.41:1.8: dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.41:1.9: Imesu, bhikkhave, catūsu satipaṭṭhānesu suppatiṭṭhitacittā viharatha.

translation_text.sn47.41:1.2: “Mendicants, you should meditate with your mind firmly established in the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn47.41:1.3: Don’t let freedom from death escape you.
translation_text.sn47.41:1.5: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.41:1.6: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings …
translation_text.sn47.41:1.8: principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.41:1.9: You should meditate with your mind firmly established in the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn47.41:1.10: Don’t let freedom from death escape you.”
